journey.name: Load descriptor via QR scan
journey.description:
Opens the descriptor QR scanner from Settings, falls back to the paste path (the scanner sheet is a bottom sheet, and an emulator/device may not have a usable camera), submits a wallet descriptor, confirms it in the confirmation dialog, and verifies the descriptor list updates without the app crashing.
IMPORTANT — network match: a descriptor only loads if its extended key matches the node's network. A mainnet node (chain "bitcoin") rejects a testnet `tpub`, and vice versa, with an "Invalid descriptor … Error while parsing xkey" snackbar. So this journey first reads the node's network and then types a network-appropriate descriptor. Mainnet uses the `xpub` descriptor below; signet/testnet/testnet4/ regtest all use the `tpub` descriptor below.
Camera-dependent steps are SKIPPABLE when the device has no camera. The paste fallback inside the sheet and the confirmation dialog are rendered in separate windows, so their controls are targeted by visible text, not testTag.

actions.action: Launch the Mandacaru app and wait for the splash screen to dismiss (poll until nav_node is present)
Tap the Node Info navigation item (nav_node) and read the node's network from node_network (e.g. "bitcoin", "signet", "testnet"); remember it for the descriptor choice below
Expand the Descriptors section if it is collapsed

If a camera permission dialog appears, grant it (tap Allow). Then verify the descriptor scan sheet opens — a camera preview titled "Scan wallet descriptor", or, if camera permission is denied/unavailable, a "Camera permission is required" fallback (SKIPPABLE: depends on device camera)
Tap the "Paste instead" button (target by text) to switch the sheet to paste mode

Tap the paste text field. If the node's network is mainnet ("bitcoin"), type "wpkh([abcd1234/84h/0h/0h]xpub6BosfCnifzxcFwrSzQiqu2DBVTshkCXacvNsWGYJVVhhawA7d4R5WSWGFNbi8Aw6ZRc1brxMyWMzG3DSSSSoekkudhUd9yLb6qx39T9nMdj/0/*)". Otherwise (signet/testnet/testnet4/regtest) type "wpkh([73c5da0a/84h/1h/0h]tpubDC8msFGeGuwnKG9Upg7DM2b4DaRqg3CUZa5g8v2SRQ6K4NSkxUgd7HsL2XVWbVm39yBA4LgAFKvDsdsBPzMw3RGYbjeMs9dGcTLeUw6f7c/0/*)"
Dismiss the soft keyboard if it covers the buttons (press Back once — this only hides the keyboard, it does not close the sheet)
Tap the "Decode" button (target by text) to submit the pasted descriptor

Verify a confirmation dialog appears titled "Load this descriptor?" showing a detected script type ("Native SegWit (P2WPKH)"), the fingerprint, the derivation path, and the full descriptor (target by text)
Tap the "Load" button in the confirmation dialog (target by text)
Verify the Descriptors list now shows the loaded descriptor (a wpkh(...) entry) and the app has not crashed
